results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import inspect
import rasa.core.events.__init__ as events

def test_useruttered_from_parse_data_has_type_hints_for_key_params__a1():
    """Ensure UserUttered._from_parse_data has explicit type hints for key params."""
    sig = inspect.signature(events.UserUttered._from_parse_data)
    text_ann = sig.parameters['text'].annotation
    parse_data_ann = sig.parameters['parse_data'].annotation
    assert text_ann is events.Text, f"Expected 'text' parameter to be annotated as Text, but got {text_ann!r}."

def test_useruttered_from_parse_data_has_type_hints_for_key_params__a2():
    """Ensure UserUttered._from_parse_data has explicit type hints for key params."""
    sig = inspect.signature(events.UserUttered._from_parse_data)
    text_ann = sig.parameters['text'].annotation
    parse_data_ann = sig.parameters['parse_data'].annotation
    assert parse_data_ann == events.Dict[events.Text, events.Any], f"Expected 'parse_data' parameter to be annotated as Dict[Text, Any], but got {parse_data_ann!r}."

def test_useruttered_from_parse_data_has_type_hints_for_key_params__a3():
    """Ensure UserUttered._from_parse_data has explicit type hints for key params."""
    sig = inspect.signature(events.UserUttered._from_parse_data)
    text_ann = sig.parameters['text'].annotation
    parse_data_ann = sig.parameters['parse_data'].annotation
    assert 'message_id' in sig.parameters, "Expected UserUttered._from_parse_data to accept 'message_id' parameter (required to pass message IDs through parsing)."

def test_useruttered_from_parse_data_has_type_hints_for_key_params__a4():
    """Ensure UserUttered._from_parse_data has explicit type hints for key params."""
    sig = inspect.signature(events.UserUttered._from_parse_data)
    text_ann = sig.parameters['text'].annotation
    parse_data_ann = sig.parameters['parse_data'].annotation
    message_id_ann = sig.parameters['message_id'].annotation
    assert message_id_ann == events.Optional[events.Text], f"Expected 'message_id' parameter to be annotated as Optional[Text], but got {message_id_ann!r}."

results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import os
os.environ.pop('PYTEST_ADDOPTS', None)
import inspect

def test_usermessage_init_docstring_mentions_output_channel_used_to_send_responses__a1():
    import rasa.core.channels.channel as channel
    doc = inspect.getdoc(channel.UserMessage.__init__)
    assert doc is not None, 'Expected UserMessage.__init__ to have a docstring.'

def test_usermessage_init_docstring_mentions_output_channel_used_to_send_responses__a2():
    import rasa.core.channels.channel as channel
    doc = inspect.getdoc(channel.UserMessage.__init__)
    expected = 'output_channel: the output channel which should be used to send bot responses back to the user.'
    assert expected in doc, f"UserMessage.__init__ docstring should explain that 'output_channel' is used to send bot responses back to the user.\nMissing expected line:\n{expected}\n\nActual docstring:\n{doc}"
